title: Cu(OAc)2 catalyzed N-benzylation of 3-aminocoumarins with primary aromatic amines and alcohols : A new efficient route to access direct C-N bond via monobenzylation of 3-aminocoumarin derivatives
author: Nazia Kausar
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, University of Calcutta, Kolkata-700 009, India 

  E - mail  :  ardchem@caluniv.ac.in ,  ardas66@rediffmail.com     Fax  : 91-33-23519754 

  Manuscript received 07 December 2017, revised 09 January 2018, accepted 16 January 2017  

 Cu(OAc) 2  catalyzed N-benzylation of 3-aminocoumarin derivatives has been accomplished employ­ing benzylamine and benzylalcohol as benzylating agent. In contrast to the conventional methodologies em­ploying precious metal catalysis and hazardous benzyl bromide reagent, the use of earth-abundant metal catalyst and aromatic amines or alcohols as direct benzylating agents is generally an attractive and less hazardous process for benzylation of 3-aminocoumarin derivatives. Developed synthetic protocol offers various advan­tages like operational simplicity, inexpensive metal catalyst, relatively lower thermal energy and a high product yield. It is noteworthy to mention that N-benzylation of 3-aminocoumarin is limited to monobenzylation step keeping away from the possibility of over alkylation.
